data.title: Knowles Appoints Laura Angelini to Board of Directors
data.description:
Pick the best stocks and maximize your portfolio:

  • Discover top-rated stocks from highly ranked analysts with Analyst Top Stocks!
  • Easily identify outperforming stocks and invest smarter with Top Smart Score Stocks

An update from Knowles ( (KN) ) is now available.

Knowles Corporation has appointed Laura Angelini, a seasoned executive with 30 years in the medical device and healthcare industry, to its Board of Directors. With extensive experience at companies like Baxter International and Johnson & Johnson, Angelini’s insights are set to bolster Knowles’ strategic transformation towards higher-value markets and products. Her appointment aligns with Knowles’ focus on enhancing shareholder value and expanding in high-growth sectors.

Learn more about KN stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Associated British Foods Executes Share Buyback Program
data.description:
Associated British Foods (GB:ABF) has released an update.

Pick the best stocks and maximize your portfolio:

  • Discover top-rated stocks from highly ranked analysts with Analyst Top Stocks!
  • Easily identify outperforming stocks and invest smarter with Top Smart Score Stocks

Associated British Foods has executed a share buyback, purchasing 355,245 of its own shares at an average price of 2,223.8943 pence per share. This transaction, facilitated by UBS AG London Branch, is part of the company’s ongoing share repurchase program announced in November 2024. The repurchased shares are intended for cancellation, potentially boosting the stock’s value by reducing the overall share count.

For further insights into GB:ABF stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Drax Group plc has recently acquired 233,734 of its own ordinary shares as part of a buyback program initiated in August 2024. The share buyback aims to optimize capital structure and enhance shareholder value, with the weighted average purchase price noted at 648.77 pence. This transaction contributes to a total of 15.77 million shares bought back since the program’s start.

data.title: Aptose Biosciences Advances TUS as AML Therapy
data.description:
Pick the best stocks and maximize your portfolio:

  • Discover top-rated stocks from highly ranked analysts with Analyst Top Stocks!
  • Easily identify outperforming stocks and invest smarter with Top Smart Score Stocks

The latest update is out from Aptose Biosciences ( (TSE:APS) ).

Aptose Biosciences is advancing tuspetinib (TUS), a promising oral kinase inhibitor, as a frontline therapy for newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia (AML) patients ineligible for intensive chemotherapy. The TUS+VEN+AZA combination therapy shows potential in overcoming VEN resistance, offering broad efficacy and favorable safety in AML patients, including those with challenging genetic mutations. The ongoing clinical trials aim to establish a new standard of care for AML, targeting significant unmet needs in the market.

See more data about APS stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
